summary: Command-and-control backdoor with install-time and import-time persistence beaconing to 98.86.244.177:8080
details: The package contains a fully functional C2 backdoor that activates both at install time and at module load time. postinstall.js (run automatically by `npm install`) re-spawns itself as a detached daemon (POSTINSTALL_DAEMON=1), POSTs the host's UUID, hostname, and platform to http://98.86.244.177:8080/register, then polls http://98.86.244.177:8080/beacon every 30 seconds and passes any returned `command` field directly to child_process.exec, sending stdout/stderr back to /results. index.js (the package's declared main) contains a top-level IIFE with the same registration, beacon-polling, and exec-and-report loop, so any consumer that `require('friendly-greeter-demo')` instantly starts the C2 loop in their own process. Host identification (hostname + platform + generated UUID) is sent over plain HTTP to a bare-IP endpoint before any command is issued.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
